title: HLX-343: Add --mode flag to hlx tickets create
description: Walkthrough of changes adding explicit mode selection to the ticket create command, including validation, body construction, output guard, and usage text.

steps.title: Updated CreateTicketResponse type
steps.description: Made shortId optional (shortId?: string) and added mode?: string to handle backend responses that may omit shortId or include mode.

steps.title: VALID_MODES constant
steps.description: Defines the 5 allowed mode values as a const tuple: AUTO, BUILD, FIX, RESEARCH, EXECUTE. Used for validation and error messaging.

steps.title: Parse --mode flag
steps.description: Uses the existing getFlag utility (already imported but previously unused) to parse the optional --mode argument from CLI args.

steps.title: Case-insensitive mode validation
steps.description: When --mode is provided: normalizes to uppercase via .toUpperCase(), validates against VALID_MODES with .includes(). Invalid values trigger console.error with allowed values list followed by process.exit(1), matching the existing error pattern from the repositoryIds check above.

steps.title: Conditional mode in POST body
steps.description: Uses spread syntax ...(mode && { mode }) to include the mode field only when provided. When mode is undefined, the spread produces nothing — the body is identical to the original { title, description, repositoryIds }.

steps.title: shortId output guard
steps.description: Uses nullish coalescing (data.ticket.shortId ?? '(pending)') to prevent printing 'Short ID: undefined' when the backend omits the shortId field.

steps.title: Conditional mode display
steps.description: Displays the mode in success output only when present in the API response, following the same conditional pattern used for data.run display.

steps.title: Updated usage text
steps.description: Added [--mode <AUTO|BUILD|FIX|RESEARCH|EXECUTE>] to the tickets create usage line, showing it as optional with all 5 allowed values.
